subject: Anthropology
title: Domestic Mode of Production
description: A fast breakdown of Domestic Mode of Production for builders.

hook.text: Why would a farmer stop planting when he could grow more? Because he has enough. Not every economy seeks surplus.

buildup.text: Sahlins described the domestic mode of production — households producing for their own needs, not for profit or market.

discovery.text: In this system, families produce what they need and stop. There's no drive to accumulate beyond sufficiency.

twist.text: Chiefs and states push production beyond household needs. Surplus doesn't arise naturally — it's demanded by power.

climax.text: The domestic mode shows that overproduction isn't human nature. The drive to produce more is a political creation.

punchline.text: Enough is a revolutionary economic concept.

quiz.question: What is the domestic mode of production?
options: Households producing for their own needs, not for profit
